message:
fix(frankenterm-core): restore 3 parent-crate replay modules referenced by lib.rs (ft-y0loj.4 / ft-j1qjt.2 partial-extraction completion)

Adds the 3 source files in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/` whose
`pub mod` declarations were already restored to the parent crate's
`lib.rs` but whose source files were missing on disk under git, and
syncs `Cargo.lock` to register the `frankenterm-core-replay` package
that the parent crate already depends on.

Background:
- Commit b7c9f64a removed `replay.rs`, `replay_capture.rs`, and
  `replay_fixture_harvest.rs` from the parent crate during the first
  attempt at the ft-j1qjt replay extraction.
- Commit baef663e reverted that extraction ("ft-j1qjt replay
  extraction blocked — not a tier-1 leaf"), restoring lib.rs's
  `pub mod replay; pub mod replay_capture; pub mod replay_fixture_harvest;`
  declarations.
- Commit 9bbb2a60 then re-extracted only the safe portion (24 of 28
  modules) into the new `frankenterm-core-replay` sub-crate. The
  4 modules that DIDN'T move stayed in the parent: `recorder_replay`
  (already tracked) and these 3, which were left on disk but never
  re-added to the index. The result: HEAD's lib.rs declares modules
  whose source files are not in git — a clone would fail to build.

This commit re-checks in the 3 missing files, verbatim, completing
the "24 of 28 extracted, 4 stay in parent" partial-extraction story
documented in `docs/proposals/ft-j1qjt-replay-tier1-blocker.md`.

Files restored:

- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/replay.rs` (~83 KB / 2,514 LOC)
    Replay engine for `.war` (wa-recording) session recordings.
    Frame parsing (14-byte binary header: timestamp_ms / frame_type /
    flags / payload_len), keyframe management (default interval 50),
    speed control, pause/resume, and seek. Uses
    `crate::recording::{FrameHeader, FrameType, RecordingFrame,
    checked_frame_payload_len}` for the frame layout and
    `crate::runtime_async::{sleep, watch}` for the async timing
    primitives — both are within-crate edges, so this module is a
    legitimate parent-crate resident, not a candidate for the
    sub-crate.

- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/replay_capture.rs` (~51 KB / 1,568 LOC)
    Replay-capture adapter that taps the ingest/event pipeline and
    emits `.ftreplay`-compatible RecorderEvent records with
    deterministic event IDs and merge keys. Designed as an
    observer (zero-cost when no sink is attached). Pulls types from
    the within-crate `ingest`, `events`, and `recorder_replay`
    modules — those couplings are why this module had to stay in
    the parent crate per the ft-j1qjt blocker analysis.

- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/replay_fixture_harvest.rs` (~129 KB / 3,784 LOC)
    Replay-fixture harvesting pipeline (bead `ft-og6q6.7.1`) that
    turns real incident/swarm captures into curated `.ftreplay`
    artifacts with deterministic redaction, sensitivity tagging,
    quality filters, duplicate overlap detection, and registry
    persistence keyed by incident/risk tags. Like `replay_capture`,
    its imports cross into `crate::recorder_replay`, `crate::events`,
    `crate::policy`, and `crate::workflows`, which is the structural
    reason it stayed parent-side.

Cargo.lock change (paired with the restoration):
- Adds the `frankenterm-core-replay` package entry to the
  `[[package]]` block list:
        [[package]]
        name = "frankenterm-core-replay"
        version = "0.1.0"
        dependencies = [
          "chrono", "flate2", "frankenterm-core",
          "frankenterm-core-replay-types", "hex", "proptest",
          "serde", "serde_json", "sha2 0.10.9", "tempfile",
          "thiserror 2.0.18", "toml 0.8.23", "tracing",
        ]
- Adds `"frankenterm-core-replay"` to the parent
  `frankenterm-core` package's resolved `dependencies` list.
- This is purely a lockfile catch-up. The path-dep edge
  `frankenterm-core-replay = { path = "../frankenterm-core-replay" }`
  was already added to `crates/frankenterm-core/Cargo.toml` in
  commit 9bbb2a60. Cargo had not been re-run since then to update
  the lock file, so this commit produces the missing
  resolved-package entries.

What this commit does NOT do:
- Does NOT change any of the existing 24 sub-crate replay modules.
- Does NOT alter `lib.rs` (the `pub mod replay*` declarations were
  already correct on HEAD).
- Does NOT modify `recorder_replay.rs` (the 4th parent-resident
  module, already tracked).
- Does NOT introduce new dependencies — all dep edges in
  Cargo.lock already had source-level declarations.

Cross-references:
- ft-y0loj.4 — replay-cluster strangler-fig migration parent.
- ft-j1qjt.2 — "extract 24 of 28 replay_* modules" sub-task.
- docs/proposals/ft-j1qjt-replay-tier1-blocker.md — analysis
  explaining why these 4 modules must stay in the parent crate
  (cross-edges into ingest/events/recorder/policy/workflows).
- Companion sibling commit on this branch:
  feat(workspace): add frankenterm-core-ars sub-crate sources
  (ft-y0loj.2) — same pattern at a different cluster.

Co-author note: parallel-agent contributions in this session
created the on-disk files; committed verbatim per AGENTS.md's
"treat parallel-agent changes as your own" rule.
